Project

  • Changelog
diskripr
  • API Reference
  • Models
  • View page source

Models

Data models that flow between diskripr pipeline stages.

All types are plain dataclasses. No Pydantic — data is populated by driver code that has already validated tool output during parsing. Occasional __post_init__ guards handle invariants (e.g. year must be a 4-digit int).

Defined types:

  • DriveInfo — Optical drive device path and MakeMKV drive index.

  • DiscInfo — Drive info, disc title string, and title list.

  • Title — Per-title metadata from a MakeMKV scan: index, name,

    duration (HH:MM:SS), size in bytes, chapter count, stream summary, and a heuristic type tag ("main", "feature-length", "extra", "short"). Computed property duration_seconds.

  • Selection — The chosen main Title plus a list of

    ClassifiedExtra objects.

  • ClassifiedExtra — A Title reference annotated with a Jellyfin extra

    type (e.g. "behindthescenes") and the generated output filename.

  • RipResult — Outcome of ripping a single title: title index,

    output path, success flag, error message.

  • EncodeResult — Same shape as RipResult plus original and encoded

    file sizes in bytes.

  • VideoTrack — Codec name and resolution string.

  • AudioTrack — Codec name, language code, and channel count.

  • SubtitleTrack — Codec name, language code, track title, and forced

    flag.

  • StreamReport — Aggregated video, audio, and subtitle tracks for a

    single MKV file.

class diskripr.models.DriveInfo(device: str, drive_index: int)[source]

Bases: object

Optical drive device path and MakeMKV drive index.

device: str
drive_index: int
class diskripr.models.DiscInfo(drive: DriveInfo, disc_title: str, titles: list[Title] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Drive info plus the disc title string returned by MakeMKV.

drive: DriveInfo
disc_title: str
titles: list[Title]
class diskripr.models.Title(index: int, name: str, duration: str, size_bytes: int, chapter_count: int, stream_summary: str, title_type: Literal['main', 'feature-length', 'extra', 'short'])[source]

Bases: object

Per-title metadata from a MakeMKV scan.

index: int
name: str
duration: str
size_bytes: int
chapter_count: int
stream_summary: str
title_type: Literal['main', 'feature-length', 'extra', 'short']
property duration_seconds: int

Return duration as total seconds.

class diskripr.models.ClassifiedExtra(title: Title, extra_type: Literal['behindthescenes', 'deletedscene', 'featurette', 'interview', 'scene', 'short', 'trailer', 'extra'], output_filename: str)[source]

Bases: object

A Title annotated with a Jellyfin extra type and output filename.

title: Title
extra_type: Literal['behindthescenes', 'deletedscene', 'featurette', 'interview', 'scene', 'short', 'trailer', 'extra']
output_filename: str
class diskripr.models.Selection(main: Title, extras: list[ClassifiedExtra] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

The chosen main Title plus any classified extras.

main: Title
extras: list[ClassifiedExtra]
class diskripr.models.RipResult(title_index: int, output_path: Path | None, success: bool, error_message: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Outcome of ripping a single title.

title_index: int
output_path: Path | None
success: bool
error_message: str | None = None
class diskripr.models.EncodeResult(title_index: int, output_path: Path | None, success: bool, error_message: str | None = None, original_size_bytes: int | None = None, encoded_size_bytes: int | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Outcome of encoding a single title, including before/after file sizes.

title_index: int
output_path: Path | None
success: bool
error_message: str | None = None
original_size_bytes: int | None = None
encoded_size_bytes: int | None = None
class diskripr.models.VideoTrack(codec: str, resolution: str)[source]

Bases: object

Codec name and resolution string for a video stream.

codec: str
resolution: str
class diskripr.models.AudioTrack(codec: str, language: str, channels: int)[source]

Bases: object

Codec name, language code, and channel count for an audio stream.

codec: str
language: str
channels: int
class diskripr.models.SubtitleTrack(codec: str, language: str, track_title: str, forced: bool)[source]

Bases: object

Codec name, language code, track title, and forced flag for a subtitle stream.

codec: str
language: str
track_title: str
forced: bool
class diskripr.models.StreamReport(video_tracks: list[VideoTrack] = <factory>, audio_tracks: list[AudioTrack] = <factory>, subtitle_tracks: list[SubtitleTrack] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated stream tracks for a single MKV file.

video_tracks: list[VideoTrack]
audio_tracks: list[AudioTrack]
subtitle_tracks: list[SubtitleTrack]
